unicourt_analysis:
- The average case duration for personal injury cases across all counties is 669 days.
- The shortest average time to resolution for personal injury cases is in Los Angeles County, with 507 days.
- Bronx County shows the fastest docket closure rates for personal injury claims, with the shortest average case duration of 576 days overall.
- Motions to dismiss are granted at an average success rate of 19.49% across all counties.
- The success rate for summary judgment motions is highest in Los Angeles County at 75.15%.
- The average time-to-dismissal for personal injury cases is 680 days across all counties.
- The average duration for personal injury cases that do not proceed to trial is shortest in Bronx County at 50 days overall.

**TAKEAWAY:** Los Angeles County offers the best predictability for cycle time and highest success in summary judgment motions, while Bronx County leads in fastest docket closure. Motions to dismiss are less frequently successful overall, suggesting a strategic focus on summary judgment in LA may yield better outcomes.
coaction_analysis:
- The average case duration for Coaction personal injury cases is 730 days across all counties.
- The shortest average time to resolution for Coaction cases is in Los Angeles County with 699 days.
- Bronx County shows the fastest docket closure rates for Coaction cases with an average duration of 615 days.
- Motions to dismiss for Coaction cases have an average success rate of 41.67% across all counties.
- Summary judgment motions for Coaction cases have a 100% success rate overall.
- The average time-to-dismissal for Coaction cases is 605 days across all counties.
- The average duration for Coaction personal injury cases that do not proceed to trial is 53 days overall.

**TAKEAWAY:** Coaction cases generally take longer than market averages, especially in Kings and Los Angeles Counties, despite strong success rates in motions to dismiss and summary judgments. Bronx County remains the most efficient venue for Coaction case resolution, indicating potential operational improvements in other jurisdictions.

takeaways.Average Case Duration for Personal Injur: Coaction cases take approximately 9% longer than the overall market average, indicating room for operational efficiency improvements, especially in Los Angeles County where Coaction’s duration is notably higher than market norms.
takeaways.Shortest Case Duration to Resolution for: Coaction’s shortest resolution times lag behind the market by about 10%, particularly in Kings County, suggesting a need for enhanced early resolution strategies to close the gap with overall jurisdiction performance.
takeaways.Count of Motions to Dismiss for Personal: New York jurisdictions (Kings and Bronx) see higher volumes of motions to dismiss compared to Los Angeles County, reflecting regional litigation practices and judicial openness to early case termination, while Coaction files very few such motions.
takeaways.Average Success Rate of Motions to Dismi: Motions to dismiss are significantly more successful in New York counties than in Los Angeles County, with Coaction achieving higher success rates than the overall market, indicating that dismissal strategies are more viable in NY venues.
takeaways.Average Case Duration to Dismissal for P: Coaction’s time-to-dismissal is shorter than the overall market average, but still shows variability across jurisdictions; improvements in Kings County dismissal timing could enhance case management efficiency.
takeaways.Average Success Rate of Summary Judgment: Summary judgment motions are highly effective in Los Angeles County and for Coaction cases, underscoring their strategic importance in resolving personal injury claims efficiently.
takeaways.Average Case Duration (No Trial) for Per: Cases resolved without trial conclude fastest in Bronx County across both market and Coaction data, highlighting efficient non-trial resolution processes in that jurisdiction.
takeaways.Count of Cases Resolved for Personal Inj: Kings County resolves the highest volume of personal injury cases overall, followed by Los Angeles and Bronx Counties, reflecting differences in court capacity and throughput that impact case cycle times and backlog management.
